summary: The browser-based reproduction confirms that attacker-controlled HTML passed to `html2pdf().from(..., 'string')` reaches live DOM insertion and executes an inline `onerror` handler.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should flip a proof marker in the page by executing attacker-controlled JavaScript from the supplied HTML string.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction printed `{"triggered":true,"status":"triggered","marker":"yes"}`, confirming that the payload executed and modified the page state in the vulnerable origin.
verification_method: The run script serves a self-contained PoC page, drives a headless browser to load it, and asserts that a DOM marker and status text were changed by the payload itself rather than by unrelated page code.
evidence_produced_by_run: The browser automation output reported `triggered: true`.
The page status text changed from `not-triggered` to `triggered`.
The payload set `document.body[data-pwned=yes]`, which the verifier read back.
